  name being parsed as an env var

commit.message:
fix: replace unguarded import Darwin with canImport(Darwin)/Glibc guards

Darwin is Apple-only; on Linux the equivalent is Glibc. Replace unguarded
import Darwin with #if canImport(Darwin) / #elseif canImport(Glibc) in:
- WaxMCPServer/main.swift #else fallback block
- WaxRepo/main.swift #else fallback block
- WaxCLI/AsyncRunner.swift

Also remove Darwin module qualifier from exit() calls so the unqualified
global exit() resolves correctly on both Darwin and Glibc.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve Linux os module unavailability in Wax target

- Replace OSAllocatedUnfairLock with NSLock in NativeBpeTokenizer.swift;
  the os module is not available on Linux, but NSLock (Foundation) is
  cross-platform and functionally equivalent for this cache lock use case
- Guard import os and Logger usage in WaxDiagnostics.swift with
  #if canImport(os); provide FileHandle.standardError fallback for Linux

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove unused CoreGraphics import from PhotoRAGTypes.swift

CoreGraphics is not available on Linux; the import was unused since
PhotoRAGTypes.swift only uses standard Swift types (Double, Int, String).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve Linux build failures for Wax target compilation

- Wrap FTS5Serializer.swift in #if canImport(SQLite3) with Linux stubs;
  SQLite3 system module requires libsqlite3-dev which is not installed on
  Linux CI; GRDB bundles its own SQLite but does not expose the module map
- Add matching #if canImport(SQLite3) guards in FTS5SerializerTests.swift,
  StructuredMemorySchemaTests.swift, and TextSearchEngineTests.swift which
  use raw SQLite3 C API for blob inspection
- Wrap PhotoRAGOrchestrator.swift, PhotosAssetMetadata.swift,
  PhotoRAGProtocols.swift, MultimodalEmbeddingProvider.swift, and
  VideoRAGOrchestrator.swift in #if canImport(ImageIO) so that CGImage
  and ImageIO APIs (Apple-only) are not compiled on Linux
- Add #if canImport(Accelerate) conditional to VectorMath.swift with
  scalar loop fallbacks for Linux; vDSP_* calls require Accelerate which
  is not available on Linux
- Remove unused `import Accelerate` from NativeBpeTokenizer.swift

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve Linux CFAbsoluteTimeGetCurrent and macOS rg CI failures

- BertTokenizer.swift: replace CFAbsoluteTimeGetCurrent() with
  Date().timeIntervalSinceReferenceDate in Utils struct. CFAbsoluteTimeGetCurrent
  is a CoreFoundation function unavailable on Linux; Date().timeIntervalSinceReferenceDate
  is pure Swift Foundation and equivalent on all platforms.

- check_corruption_assertions.sh: replace rg (ripgrep) with grep.
  The macOS CI runner does not have ripgrep installed, so all three rg
  invocations returned 'command not found', causing corruption_matches to
  be empty and the test-count check to fail even though the tests exist.
  grep -E and grep -q are standard POSIX tools available everywhere.

Co-authored-by: Christopher Karani <christopherkarani@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve Linux Accelerate import, restore pre-commit vector search, and fix CI timeout

- Move `import Accelerate` inside `#if canImport(CoreML)` guard in
  MiniLMEmbeddings.swift so Linux builds no longer fail with
  "no such module 'Accelerate'"
- Revert WaxSession.search to pass `vectorEngine: nil` (not the
  pre-initialized session engine) so UnifiedSearchEngineCache is
  consulted, enabling pending embeddings to be visible before commit.
  The stale pre-initialized engine was causing two test failures:
  unifiedSession_vectorSearchWorksBeforeAndAfterCommit and
  metalVectorSearchNormalizesNonNormalizedQueryEmbedding
- Increase waitForScheduledReport timeout from 20s to 90s in
  scheduledLiveSetRewriteFlushTriggerRunsDeferredFromCommitPath
  to accommodate CI runners where the .utility-priority background
  maintenance task can take longer than 20s to complete

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve USearchVectorEngine Sendable errors and Linux CoreML build failures

- USearchVectorEngine.swift: add T: Sendable to withWriteLock/withReadLock
  (fixes Swift 6 strict-concurrency error on macOS CI)
- WaxVectorSearchMiniLM/CoreML/BertTokenizer.swift: wrap CoreML-dependent
  code in #if canImport(CoreML)...#endif; Foundation-only classes remain
  unconditional
- WaxVectorSearchMiniLM/CoreML/MiniLMEmbeddings.swift: wrap with
  #if canImport(CoreML)...#endif
- WaxVectorSearchMiniLM/CoreML/all-MiniLM-L6-v2.swift: wrap with
  #if canImport(CoreML)...#endif
- WaxVectorSearchMiniLM/MiniLMEmbedder.swift: wrap entire body in
  #if canImport(CoreML)...#endif (also guards OSLog import)
- MemoryOrchestrator+MiniLM.swift, MemoryOrchestrator+Prewarm.swift:
  change canImport(WaxVectorSearchMiniLM) to
  canImport(WaxVectorSearchMiniLM) && canImport(CoreML) so MiniLMEmbedder
  references compile only when CoreML is available
- WaxIntegrationTests MiniLM test files: add && canImport(CoreML) to all
  #if canImport(WaxVectorSearchMiniLM) guards that reference CoreML types
  (MLMultiArray, MiniLMEmbedder, MiniLMEmbeddings)

Co-authored-by: Christopher Karani <christopherkarani@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve concurrency Sendable errors and Linux ObjectiveC build failure

- Add T: Sendable constraint to AsyncReadWriteLock.withReadLock/withWriteLock
  in ReadWriteLock.swift to satisfy Swift 6 strict concurrency requirements
- Add T: Sendable constraint to AsyncMutex.withLock for the same reason
- Guard import ObjectiveC and ObjC-based buffer serialization methods in
  USearchSendable.swift with #if canImport(ObjectiveC); add #else fallback
  using file-based save(path:)/load(path:) so Linux builds compile cleanly

Co-authored-by: Christopher Karani <christopherkarani@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve concurrency errors and Linux build failures

- Add T: Sendable constraints to withWriteLock/withReadLock in Wax.swift
  and MetalVectorEngine.swift to satisfy Swift 6 strict-concurrency rules
- Wrap MetalVectorEngine.swift in #if canImport(Metal) so Linux builds
  no longer fail with "no such module 'Metal'"
- Guard all MetalVectorEngine references in VectorSearchSession.swift,
  WaxSession.swift, UnifiedSearch.swift, and UnifiedSearchEngineCache.swift
  with #if canImport(Metal) compile-time conditions
- Guard Metal-specific tests in VectorSearchEngineTests.swift with
  #if canImport(Metal); the import Metal line is also made conditional
- Fix Package.swift indentation for the swift-testing dependency entry
- Extend parseObjectValue in FactsCommand.swift to accept scientific
  notation doubles (e.g. 1e5, 1.5E10) in addition to decimal notation

Co-authored-by: Christopher Karani <christopherkarani@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove swift-tiktoken dependency and fix remaining review issues

- Remove swift-tiktoken from Package.swift deps and Package.resolved; it
  required swift-tools-version 6.2 (breaking Swift 6.1 runners) and used
  URLSession.shared which does not exist on Linux.
- Simplify TokenCounter.swift to use only NativeBpeTokenizer (pure Swift,
  cross-platform). Removes CoreBPECache, compare backend, and tiktoken
  backend; keeps LRU cache, batch operations, and public API intact.
- Update DependencyTests.swift: remove SwiftTiktoken import and the
  tiktokenSwiftLoadsTypeInfo test.
- Rewrite NativeBpeTokenizerTests.swift: remove tiktoken comparison
  tests; add standalone encode/decode round-trip and empty-input tests.
- Rename memvid.embedding.* metadata keys to wax.embedding.* in
  WaxSession.swift to remove stale pre-rename namespace.
- Fix compiler warning in Wax.swift:937: change
  `catch let WaxError.capacityExceeded(_, _)` to
  `catch WaxError.capacityExceeded` (no-op let binding silenced).

Co-authored-by: Christopher Karani <christopherkarani@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address all review issues from MCP release PR

Co-authored-by: Christopher Karani <christopherkarani@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix 30-second demo crash: auto-fallback to text-only when no embedder provided

When MemoryOrchestrator is created without an EmbeddingProvider and no
pre-existing vector index exists in the store, automatically disable
vector search instead of throwing an error. This makes the simple
`MemoryOrchestrator(at:)` initializer work out-of-the-box with BM25
text search. Users who want hybrid vector+text search can use
`MemoryOrchestrator.openMiniLM(at:)` or pass their own embedder.

Updates the README 30-second demo to clarify the text-only default and
show the MiniLM upgrade path.

Closes #34

https://claude.ai/code/session_01X25YygEqmZtSTmeVYxyhRy

When MemoryOrchestrator is created without an EmbeddingProvider and no
pre-existing vector index exists in the store, automatically disable
vector search instead of throwing an error. This makes the simple
`MemoryOrchestrator(at:)` initializer work out-of-the-box with BM25

commit.message:
Fix MCP server bugs: photo init waste, malformed JSON, unsafe arithmetic, signal handling

- BUG-2: Stop initializing PhotoRAGOrchestrator since photo tools are
  permanently stubbed ("Requires Soju"). Removes wasted startup time,
  memory, and unnecessary photoURL resolution/shutdown flush.

- BUG-5: Non-finite doubles (NaN/Infinity) now return descriptive strings
  instead of silently converting to null in JSON responses.

- BUG-6: Error result JSON fallback now uses escapeJSONString() helper
  to properly escape quotes, backslashes, and control characters instead
  of raw string interpolation that could produce malformed JSON.

- BUG-7: Replace wrapping arithmetic (&+) with checked (+) in remember
  handler. Frame counts can't realistically overflow Int on 64-bit; if
  they did, crashing is the correct behavior.

- GAP-10: Add SIGINT/SIGTERM signal handlers via DispatchSource so the
  server triggers graceful shutdown (flush + close) instead of abrupt
  termination that risks data loss.

- BUG-1: Document that MemoryOrchestrator.recall() doesn't accept a
  limit parameter (upstream API limitation); post-hoc truncation noted.

- Update ANALYSIS-wax-mcp.md with fix statuses, correct BUG-3 and BUG-4
  (not actual bugs after API investigation).

- Add TEST-PLAN-mcp.md with prioritized test cases for next session.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01T7bMQE1PPjcAcvQhNtC88y

verification.payload:
tree c727b253ec109b3f7961e1b0d31d9235383791b1
parent facb8b92507cc52c28085b8010667fa493863843
author Claude <noreply@anthropic.com> 1771788167 +0000
committer Claude <noreply@anthropic.com> 1771788167 +0000

Fix MCP server bugs: photo init waste, malformed JSON, unsafe arithmetic, signal handling

- BUG-2: Stop initializing PhotoRAGOrchestrator since photo tools are
  permanently stubbed ("Requires Soju"). Removes wasted startup time,
  memory, and unnecessary photoURL resolution/shutdown flush.

- BUG-5: Non-finite doubles (NaN/Infinity) now return descriptive strings
  instead of silently converting to null in JSON responses.

- BUG-6: Error result JSON fallback now uses escapeJSONString() helper
  to properly escape quotes, backslashes, and control characters instead
  of raw string interpolation that could produce malformed JSON.

- BUG-7: Replace wrapping arithmetic (&+) with checked (+) in remember
  handler. Frame counts can't realistically overflow Int on 64-bit; if
  they did, crashing is the correct behavior.

- GAP-10: Add SIGINT/SIGTERM signal handlers via DispatchSource so the
  server triggers graceful shutdown (flush + close) instead of abrupt
  termination that risks data loss.

- BUG-1: Document that MemoryOrchestrator.recall() doesn't accept a
  limit parameter (upstream API limitation); post-hoc truncation noted.

- Update ANALYSIS-wax-mcp.md with fix statuses, correct BUG-3 and BUG-4
  (not actual bugs after API investigation).

- Add TEST-PLAN-mcp.md with prioritized test cases for next session.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01T7bMQE1PPjcAcvQhNtC88y

commit.message:
Fix scanInternal decode-failure behavior and recall overload duplication

- WALRingReader.scanInternal: re-throw decode errors on checksum-validated
  records instead of silently breaking. Aligns with scanPendingMutationsWithState
  which already throws walCorruption on the same condition. A checksum pass
  followed by a decode failure indicates a codec invariant violation, not a
  recoverable partial write.

- MemoryOrchestrator.recall(query:embeddingPolicy:): collapse the two duplicate
  ragBuilder.build() calls into one. FastRAGContextBuilder.build() already
  accepts embedding as [Float]? = nil, so the if-let branch was unnecessary.

Co-authored-by: Christopher Karani <christopherkarani@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address all code review issues from PR reliability hardening

- Remove default.profraw binary artifact and add *.profraw/*.profdata to .gitignore
- WALRingReader: separate pending-entry decode from state-position scan — decode
  errors now set a stopDecodingPendingMutations flag and continue the cursor scan
  so writePos/pendingBytes remain accurate for WAL recovery
- WALRingWriter: write a best-effort cleanup sentinel at snapshot.writePos on
  faultAndRestore to prevent stale on-disk content from being mistaken for a
  valid record after a partial write failure
- PayloadCompressor: wire up Linux LZ4 C shims (wax_lz4_compress/decompress) —
  the .lz4 case now calls linuxLZ4Compress/linuxLZ4Decompress instead of throwing
- WaxCrashHarness: capture actual frameId returned by wax.put() in seedStore()
  instead of hardcoding frameId 0
- MemoryOrchestrator: extract shared buildRecallContext() helper so all four
  recall() overloads funnel through one ragConfigForRecall/recordAccessesIfEnabled
  call site, preventing silent divergence in future edits
- ReadWriteLock: replace Darwin os_unfair_lock + usleep spin with pthread_rwlock_t
  on all platforms for genuine reader concurrency
- ToolSchemas: fix indentation of Tool items inside append(contentsOf:) blocks
- Remove 20 identical CLAUDE.md stubs from Sources/ and Tests/ subdirectories
- maybeCrashAfterCheckpoint: add comment explaining the fatalError fallback intent
  for sandboxed environments where SIGKILL may be delayed

Co-authored-by: Christopher Karani <christopherkarani@users.noreply.github.com>
Add Wax reliability hardening plan

Fix review issues: remove profraw/CLAUDE.md artifacts, document WAL behavior, fix orphaned frame risk

- Remove default.profraw (profiling artifact) and add *.profraw to .gitignore
- Remove sub-directory CLAUDE.md files per policy (Sources/Wax/RAG, Stats, WaxMCPServer)
- WALRingReader: add comment explaining why decode failure throws (vs break) — checksum
  validation already handles partial writes, so a post-checksum decode failure signals
  a codec invariant violation that cannot be safely skipped
- MemoryOrchestrator.persistAccessStatsIfNeeded: fix orphaned frame risk by updating
  accessStatsFrameId before calling supersede; swallow supersede errors so next flush
  can still supersede the current frame rather than a stale one
- FastRAGContextBuilder: add comment documenting the nowMs 3-way fallback chain and
  its implications for direct callers without deterministicNowMs set
- ToolSchemas: note that wax_flush must be called to persist wax_handoff entries

Co-authored-by: Christopher Karani <christopherkarani@users.noreply.github.com>
Review Wax repo structure

commit.message: chore: remove non-root CLAUDE.md files
fixes

Add WAL embedding codec tests and replace per-element loops with bulk copy

putEmbedding encode/decode had zero test coverage and used verbose
per-element float serialization. Add 6 tests (round-trip, byte-level
wire format, 384-dim MiniLM path, edge cases, special floats,
supersedeFrame) then replace the loops with Data(buffer:) on encode
and copyMemory on decode. Wire format unchanged — pinned by
putEmbeddingByteLevelLayout test. 179/179 WaxCore tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Guard XCTest-based integration benchmarks for non-XCTest toolchains

Wrap XCTest-dependent benchmark/test files with canImport(XCTest) guards so toolchains without XCTest compile cleanly.

Fixes #28.
Prepare waxmcp package release

releases.body: Follow-up release including latest commits on feat/wax-v2-improvements through ea59ac89.

## What's Changed
* chore: update .gitignore to exclude local agent/IDE config by @christopherkarani in https://github.com/christopherkarani/Wax/pull/40
* docs: add architecture visualization and rename binaries to wax-cli/w… by @christopherkarani in https://github.com/christopherkarani/Wax/pull/41


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/christopherkarani/Wax/compare/0.1.8...0.1.9

## What's Changed
* Add Claude Code GitHub Workflow by @christopherkarani in https://github.com/christopherkarani/Wax/pull/19
* Fix VideoRAG integration test determinism and thumbnail budget assert… by @christopherkarani in https://github.com/christopherkarani/Wax/pull/20
* clean by @christopherkarani in https://github.com/christopherkarani/Wax/pull/21
* Mcp feature by @christopherkarani in https://github.com/christopherkarani/Wax/pull/23
* Add swift-testing dependency for test targets by @christopherkarani in https://github.com/christopherkarani/Wax/pull/25
* Rewrite README to remove AI-generated tone by @christopherkarani in https://github.com/christopherkarani/Wax/pull/27
* Guard XCTest integration benchmarks on non-XCTest toolchains by @christopherkarani in https://github.com/christopherkarani/Wax/pull/29
* Harden Wax reliability and determinism by @christopherkarani in https://github.com/christopherkarani/Wax/pull/32
* Fix WAL fault handling and deterministic recall paths by @christopherkarani in https://github.com/christopherkarani/Wax/pull/31
* Auto-disable vector search when no embedder provided by @christopherkarani in https://github.com/christopherkarani/Wax/pull/36
* Add deep analysis of Wax MCP server implementation by @christopherkarani in https://github.com/christopherkarani/Wax/pull/35
* Ship Wax MCP release readiness improvements by @christopherkarani in https://github.com/christopherkarani/Wax/pull/38


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/christopherkarani/Wax/compare/0.1.7...0.1.8

readme_html:
On-device memory for iOS & macOS AI agents.

No server. No cloud. One file.

Most iOS AI apps lose their memory the moment the user closes them. Wax fixes that — giving your agents persistent, searchable, private memory that lives entirely on-device in a single portable file.

import Wax
import WaxVectorSearchMiniLM

let memory = try await MemoryOrchestrator.openMiniLM(
    at: .documentsDirectory.appending(path: "agent.wax")
)

// Store a memory
try await memory.remember("User prefers concise answers and hates bullet points.")

// Retrieve the most relevant context — semantically
let context = try await memory.recall(query: "communication preferences")

Performance

Why Wax

Building AI agents on Apple platforms means juggling Core Data for persistence, FAISS or Annoy for vector search, and a tokenizer for context budgets — none of which talk to each other. Or you spin up Chroma or Pinecone and suddenly your app has a server dependency, network calls, and a privacy story you can't tell users.

Wax packages all of it into one self-contained file:

Capability Without Wax With Wax
Document storage Core Data / SQLite ✅ Built-in
Semantic search External FAISS / Annoy ✅ Built-in (HNSW)
Full-text search Another index ✅ Built-in (BM25)
Token budgeting Manual ✅ Automatic
Crash safety You figure it out ✅ WAL + dual headers
Server required Often ✅ Never

Features

  • Hybrid retrieval — BM25 keyword search fused with HNSW vector similarity. Gets the right memory, even when wording differs.
  • On-device embeddings — Powered by MiniLM, running locally. No API calls, no latency, no cost.
  • Metal acceleration — Embedding and search use Apple Silicon GPU when available.
  • Token budgets — Set a hard limit. Wax automatically trims and compresses context to fit, every time.
  • Tiered surrogates — Store full text, a gist, or a micro-summary. Trade recall for speed at query time.
  • Single portable file — The whole memory store is one .wax file. Back it up, sync it, move it.
  • Crash-safe by design — Append-only format with write-ahead logging and dual headers. No corruption on unexpected exits.
  • Swift 6 concurrency — Fully async/await native with Sendable conformances throughout.

Installation

Swift Package Manager

// Package.swift
dependencies: [
    .package(url: "https://github.com/christopherkarani/Wax.git", from: "0.1.8")
],
targets: [
    .target(
        name: "MyApp",
        dependencies: [
            .product(name: "Wax", package: "Wax"),
            .product(name: "WaxVectorSearchMiniLM", package: "Wax")
        ]
    )
]

Or in Xcode: File → Add Package Dependencies → paste the repo URL.

MCP Installer (npm)

npx -y waxmcp@latest mcp install --scope user

Claude Code Integration

After installing the MCP server, add this to your CLAUDE.md so Claude Code uses Wax as its memory:

## Rules

1. **Session start** — call `wax_handoff_latest` to resume prior context
2. **Before answering** — call `wax_recall` to check what you already know. Always try this first.
3. **When you learn something durable** — call `wax_remember`. Worth storing: user preferences, project decisions, architectural patterns, conventions, people/roles. Not worth storing: transient debugging, one-off commands.
4. **When corrected** — call `wax_forget` with what changed (e.g. "we don't use Redux anymore")
5. **Session end** — call `wax_handoff` with summary + pending tasks

## Tools

| Tool | When |
|------|------|
| `wax_remember` | User states a preference, makes a decision, or you learn a stable pattern. `project` to scope. |
| `wax_recall` | Before answering anything that might have prior context. Use `graph: true` for relationship-aware search. |
| `wax_forget` | User corrects you or facts become outdated. Natural language or `fact_id`. |
| `wax_context` | Need the full picture of a specific entity (person, project, library). |
| `wax_reflect` | Audit what you know — entity counts, top predicates, memory health. |
| `wax_handoff` | Session ending. Pass `pending_tasks` array for continuity. |
| `wax_handoff_latest` | Session starting. Loads last handoff. |

Quick Start

import Wax
import WaxVectorSearchMiniLM

// 1. Open (or create) a memory store
let memory = try await MemoryOrchestrator.openMiniLM(
    at: .documentsDirectory.appending(path: "myagent.wax")
)

// 2. Store memories
try await memory.remember("The user's name is Alex and they live in Toronto.")
try await memory.remember("Alex dislikes formal language. Keep responses casual.")
try await memory.remember("Alex is building a habit tracker in SwiftUI.")

// 3. Retrieve relevant context for a prompt
let context = try await memory.recall(query: "how should I address the user?")
print(context.items.map(\.text))

Use Cases

  • Conversational agents that remember preferences, history, and facts across sessions
  • Note-taking apps with semantic search ("find everything I wrote about WWDC")
  • Photo & video apps that index captions and transcripts for natural-language lookup
  • Personal assistants that learn user habits without sending data off-device
  • RAG pipelines built entirely on-device for sensitive or offline-first applications

Requirements

Minimum
Swift 6.2
iOS 17.0
macOS 14.0
Xcode 16.0

Apple Silicon recommended for GPU-accelerated embedding. Intel Macs fall back to CPU seamlessly.

Comparison

Wax ChromaDB Pinecone Core Data + FAISS
On-device ✅ ❌ ❌ ✅
No server ✅ ❌ ❌ ✅
Hybrid search ✅ ✅ ✅ Manual
Token budgeting ✅ ❌ ❌ ❌
Single file ✅ ❌ ❌ ❌
Swift-native API ✅ ❌ ❌ Partial
Privacy (data stays on device) ✅ ❌ ❌ ✅

Roadmap

  • CloudKit sync (opt-in, encrypted)
  • iCloud Drive .wax document support
  • Memory clustering and deduplication
  • Quantized embedding models for smaller footprint
  • Instruments template for memory profiling

Contributing

Issues and PRs are welcome. If you're building something with Wax, open a Discussion — would love to see what you're working on.

Star History

License

Apache 2.0 © Christopher Karani
